title: 🦞 Discord - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Discord. Ready for DMs and guild channels via the official Discord gateway. Discord DMs default to pairing mode. You will need to create a new application with a bot, add the bot to your server, and pair it to OpenClaw. ### Create a Discord application and bot. Set the **Username** to whatever you call your OpenClaw agent. This is your **Bot Token** and you will need it shortly. You'll generate an invite URL with the right permissions to add the bot to your server. ### Enable Developer Mode and collect your IDs. Back in the Discord app, you need to enable Developer Mode so you can copy internal IDs. Save your **Server ID** and **User ID** alongside your Bot Token — you'll send all three to OpenClaw in the next step. For pairing to work, Discord needs to allow your bot to DM you. Use `channels.discord.applicationId` for the default account, or `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.applicationId` when you run multiple Discord bots.

Technology Encyclopedia Home >How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. # How to join the OpenClaw Discord-Telegram community. The fastest path is not DIY. It is a clean template and a tight onboarding loop. is a practical question — let’s answer it in a way you can deploy. Channels act as the interface, and you can configure popular messaging integrations via the template panel or the onboarding wizard depending on the channel. ## The practical deployment path (Lighthouse). If you want a clean, reproducible setup, the most efficient route is the OpenClaw application template on Tencent Cloud Lighthouse. Even if the template handles day-one setup, you still need a repeatable way to configure models, connect channels, install skills, and keep the gateway alive. When you are ready to run it 24/7 in a clean, isolated environment, start here again: Tencent Cloud Lighthouse Special Offer. * Confirm the agent is running as a background service (not tied to your SSH session).

title: 🦞 Telegram - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Telegram. Long polling is the default mode; webhook mode is optional. Default DM policy for Telegram is pairing. ### Create the bot token in BotFather. `{channels: {telegram: { enabled: true, botToken: "123:abc", dmPolicy: "pairing", groups: { "*": { requireMention: true } },},},}`. Telegram does **not** use `openclaw channels login telegram`; configure token in config/env, then start gateway. ### Add the bot to a group. Add the bot to your group, then get both IDs that group access needs:. For first-time setup, get the group chat ID from `openclaw logs --follow`, a forwarded-ID bot, or Bot API `getUpdates`. After the group is allowed, `/whoami@<bot_username>` can confirm the user and group IDs. Negative Telegram supergroup IDs that start with `-100` are group chat IDs. Put them under `channels.telegram.groups`, not under `groupAllowFrom`. Practical pattern for one-owner bots: set your user ID in `channels.telegram.allowFrom`, leave `groupAllowFrom` unset, and allow the target groups under `channels.telegram.groups`.

title: 🦞 The Complete OpenClaw Guide: How We Run an AI Agent in ...
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# The Complete OpenClaw Guide: How We Run an AI Agent in Production (2026). Everything we've learned running OpenClaw in production at Context Studios — from installation and configuration to advanced multi-agent workflows, browser automation, and 134 MCP tools. The definitive OpenClaw guide for 2026. Here's everything we've learned running it in production — from installation to advanced multi-agent workflows. ## What is OpenClaw? OpenClaw is an open-source framework that turns AI language models into autonomous agents capable of running 24/7 across more than 20 messaging platforms. Think of it as the operating system for AI agents: it handles messaging, memory, tool use, scheduling, browser automation, and multi-agent coordination — so you can focus on telling the agent *what* to do rather than *how* to connect everything. From the ground up, it was designed for persistent agents with memory, scheduling, tool use, and multi-session management. You start it with `openclaw gateway start` and it runs in the background.

title: 🦞 How to Build and Secure a Personal AI Agent with OpenClaw
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# How to Build and Secure a Personal AI Agent with OpenClaw. But out of the box they can't check your phone bill, file an insurance rebuttal, or track your deadlines across WhatsApp, Slack, and email. It is an open-source personal AI agent that crossed 100,000 GitHub stars within its first week in late January 2026. What OpenClaw actually is, underneath the lobster mascot, is a concrete, readable implementation of every architectural pattern that powers serious production AI agents today. If you understand how it works, you understand how agentic systems work in general. In this guide, you'll learn how OpenClaw's three-layer architecture processes messages through a seven-stage agentic loop, build a working life admin agent with real configuration files, and then lock it down against the security threats most tutorials bury in a footnote. ## What Is OpenClaw? What they actually get is a **local gateway process** that runs as a background daemon on your machine or a VPS (Virtual Private Server).
